title: Cannot find value in this scope
explanation: This error occurs when you try to use a variable, function, or constant that has not been defined in the current scope. Common causes include typos, forgotten imports, or using a variable outside its scope.
error_message: error[E0425]: cannot find value `x` in this scope

fixes.description: Define the variable before using it
Check for typos in variable names
fixes.code:
let count = 5;
println!("{}", count); // Not 'coutn' or 'Count'

fixes.description: Import the item if it's from another module

tip: Rust is case-sensitive. Also check that the variable is defined in the right scope — variables defined inside a block {} are not accessible outside it.
